welcome.title: Welcome to Qdrant!
welcome.cardTitle: Get started with vector search in Qdrant
welcome.cardDescription: Start building your app by creating a collection and inserting your vectors.
welcome.sectionTitle: Connect to your Project or Start with Samples
welcome.apiDescription: Explore Qdrant's REST API and SDKs to connect, query, and manage your vector data with ease.
welcome.loadSampleDescription: Follow this tutorial to import a remote snapshot and explore vector search with real data in just a few steps.

auth.apiKeyDescription: This instance of Qdrant might be protected by an API Key. If so, please enter your API Key to continue.

tutorial.accessDenied: Access Denied: Because of the serverless mode, tutorial will not work here properly. Please contact your administrator.
tutorial.quickstartDescription: Create a collection, upsert vectors, and run a search.
tutorial.filteringBeginnerDesc: Filter search results using basic payload conditions.
tutorial.filteringAdvancedDesc: Try advanced filtering based on nested payload conditions.
tutorial.filteringFullTextDesc: Search for substrings, tokens, or phrases within text fields.
tutorial.multivectorSearchDesc: Work with data represented by ColBERT multivectors.
tutorial.sparseVectorSearchDesc: Use sparse vectors to get specific search results.
tutorial.hybridSearchDesc: Combine dense and sparse vectors for more accurate search results.
tutorial.multitenancyDesc: Manage multiple users within a single collection.

cluster.moveShardDescription: This action will move shard {{shardId}} from peer {{fromPeer}} to peer {{toPeer}}.
cluster.cancelReshardingConfirm: Are you sure you want to cancel the ongoing resharding operation for collection {{collectionName}}?

createCollection.useIdfDescription:
This checkbox enables Inverse Document Frequency (IDF) weighting.
Enabled it if you use BM25 or other models that require IDF.
createCollection.collectionNameDescription: Collection name must be unique and can contain only letters, numbers, hyphens and underscores
createCollection.globalSearchDescription:
Search across whole collection of data with optional filters. For example:
  • e-commerce search
  • website search
  • documents search
createCollection.multitenancyDescription:
Search across multiple isolated tenants. For example:
  • per-user documents
  • chat history search
  • organization-based isolation
createCollection.tenantFieldDescription: Which payload field should be used as a tenant id?
createCollection.tenantFieldLongDescription: This field should be used to filter data based on tenant id. For example: user_id, organization_id, etc. Payload field should be of a `keyword` type.
createCollection.tenantFieldPayloadDescription:
This payload field should be used to separate tenants within collection.
A specuil payload index of type `keyword` will be created for this field.
All requests to the collection should include this field as a filter.
createCollection.whatToUseForSearchDescription: There are some common configurations used for search, maybe you want to use one of them?
createCollection.simpleSingleEmbeddingDescription: Simplest configuration, only one vector field per document.
createCollection.simpleHybridSearchDescription: Dense + Sparse vectors searched simultaneously. Search covers both semantic and keyword-based search.
createCollection.customDescription: You can define your own configuration.
createCollection.configurationForDenseAndSparse: Configuration for dense and sparse embeddings
createCollection.nameOfDenseVectorField: Name of the dense vector field
createCollection.nameOfSparseVectorField: Name of the sparse vector field
createCollection.configureDenseVectors: Configure dense vectors for your collection
createCollection.vectorNameDescription: This name will be used as a name of vector
createCollection.multivectorDescription:
Create multiple sub-vectors per point.
Enabled it if you use Late Interraction models like ColBERT, ColPali, e.t.c.
createCollection.storageTierDescription:
Storage tier defines how the vector is stored.
Storage tier is optimized high data volume and low frequency of requests, performance tier is optimized for low latency
createCollection.precisionTierDescription:
Precision tier defines how vectors are compressed.
Low precision tier applies quantization, high precision tier doesn't compress vectors
createCollection.configureSparseVectors: Configure sparse vectors for your collection
createCollection.payloadIndexesDescription: We need to create indexes, if we want to do filtered search.
createCollection.keywordFieldDescription:
Keyword field index, suitable for exact match of string values.

Example: color: "red"

Docs:
createCollection.integerFieldDescription:
Integer field index, suitable for exact match and range filters on integer numbers.

Example: age: 25

Docs:
createCollection.allowMatchFiltersDescription:
This checkbox enables indexing of the integer field for exact match filters.
If enabled, index will consume additional memory.
createCollection.allowRangeFiltersDescription:
This checkbox enables indexing of the integer field for exact match filters.
If enabled, index will consume additional memory.
createCollection.floatFieldDescription:
Float field index, suitable for range filters on floating point and integer numbers.

Example: price: 99.5

Docs:
createCollection.uuidFieldDescription:
UUID field index, suitable for exact match of UUID values. Similar to keyword field, optimized for UUID values.

Example: doc_id: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"

Docs:
createCollection.datetimeFieldDescription:
Datetime field index, suitable for range filters on datetime values.

Example: created_at: "2023-02-08T10:49:00Z"

Docs:
createCollection.textFieldDescription:
Text field index, suitable for full-text filtering on string values.

Example: title: "The Last Question"

Docs:
createCollection.tokenizerDescription: Defines how the text is tokenized
createCollection.phraseMatchingDescription: Allows phrase matching at the cost of extra index structure
createCollection.geoFieldDescription:
Geo field index, suitable for geospatial filtering on latitude and longitude values.

Example: location: { "lon": 52.5200, "lat": 13.4050 }

Docs:
createCollection.boolFieldDescription:
Boolean field index, suitable for exact match of boolean values.

Example: is_active: true

Docs:
